title: Contact Center Reporting
lede: One reporting hub for your entire call operation — human agents, AI agents, dialer campaigns, and queues. Learn every KPI, chart, filter, and how to export any view to CSV in seconds.

steps.title: Open the Reporting tab
steps.content:
Everything your call center did — who called, who answered, how long it took, and what it cost — lives in one screen. Here's how to get there:

  1. In the left sidebar, click Contact Center (under the Call Center section).
  2. At the top of the page, click the Reporting tab (next to Live Monitor).
💡 The Reporting tab is visible to supervisors and admins (Monitor/QA role and above). If you don't see it, ask your administrator to raise your contact-center role — see Role Mapping.

images.alt: The Reporting tab inside Contact Center
images.caption: The Reporting tab: category pills, date presets, filters, KPI tiles, and charts — all in one view.

images.alt: The Reporting tab inside Contact Center
images.caption: The Reporting tab: category pills, date presets, filters, KPI tiles, and charts — all in one view.

steps.title: Pick a report category
steps.content:
The pill bar at the top switches between six report categories — each one slices the same call data from a different angle:

  • General — the big picture: total volume, connect and answer rates, talk time, outcomes, and daily/hourly charts.
  • Human Agents — per-agent scorecards: calls handled, handle time, availability, missed rings, and supervision activity.
  • AI Agents — per-assistant performance: containment, escalations, transfers, and cost.
  • Smart Campaigns — your AI dialer campaigns, with the same metrics grouped by campaign.
  • Human Campaigns — predictive-dialer (Human Dialer) calls grouped by dial list, with operator handle time.
  • Queue — skill-group queue transfers: waits, answer rates, and per-call outcomes.

Click any pill to switch — the date range and filters you set stay applied across all six.

images.caption: Six categories: General, Human Agents, AI Agents, Smart Campaigns, Human Campaigns, and Queue.
Six categories: General, Human Agents, AI Agents, Smart Campaigns, Human Campaigns, and Queue.

steps.title: Set the date range
steps.content:
The date controls sit in the top-right corner and apply to every category:

  1. Click a preset: Today, This week, This month (the default), Last 7 days, or Last 30 days.
  2. For an exact window, click Custom — a Start date → End date picker appears next to the presets.
  3. Click the circular Refresh button any time to reload the numbers.
💡 All times are shown in your account's timezone — the "Calls by hour of day" chart even prints the timezone in its title so there's never any guesswork.

images.alt: Date presets, custom range picker, refresh, and export buttons
images.caption: Date presets with Custom selected: a Start/End date picker appears, plus Refresh and Export.

images.alt: Date presets, custom range picker, refresh, and export buttons
images.caption: Date presets with Custom selected: a Start/End date picker appears, plus Refresh and Export.

steps.title: Narrow the data with filters
steps.content:
The filter bar under the category pills refines every tab at once. Each control does one thing:

  • All calls — limit to Inbound or Outbound only.
  • All assistants — a single AI agent.
  • All campaigns — a single campaign.
  • All owners — calls belonging to one team member's account.
  • All human agents — calls a specific human agent handled (disabled if no human agents took calls in the range).
  • All call types — e.g. web calls, phone calls, internal calls.
  • Phone number — match a caller or destination number.
  • Contact / lead — match by the CRM contact or lead on the call.
  • Call UUID — jump straight to one specific call by its ID.

When any filter is active, a Clear filters link appears at the end of the bar to reset everything in one click.

⚠️ Seeing "No calls match your filters"? A leftover filter is usually the culprit — click Clear filters before assuming there's no data.

images.caption: Filters: direction, assistant, campaign, owner, human agent, call type, phone, contact/lead, and call UUID.
Filters: direction, assistant, campaign, owner, human agent, call type, phone, contact/lead, and call UUID.

steps.title: Read the General KPI tiles
steps.content:
The General category opens with a row of KPI tiles — your call center's vital signs for the selected range:

  • Total calls — every call in the range, all sources combined.
  • Connected / Connect rate — calls that reached a live conversation.
  • Answer rate — share of calls that were picked up (by a person or a machine).
  • Voicemail rate — share that landed in voicemail.
  • Abandon rate — calls that rang a human agent or group but were never answered by a person.
  • Avg talk (AHT) — average talk time per connected call.
  • Total talk time — all talk time added up.
  • Inbound / Outbound — the direction split.
  • Total cost — combined call cost (shown when cost data is available for the range).
💡 Hover any KPI label with a dotted underline to see exactly how it's calculated.

images.caption: The General KPI row: volume, connect/answer/voicemail/abandon rates, talk time, direction split, and cost.
The General KPI row: volume, connect/answer/voicemail/abandon rates, talk time, direction split, and cost.

steps.title: Explore the General charts and panels
steps.content:
Below the KPIs, the General view packs the story of your range into a few visual panels:

  • AI agent performance, Smart campaigns, and Human campaigns — compact preview tables of each category (the dedicated tabs show the full versions).
  • Call volume by day — a bar chart of all calls vs. connected calls per day. Spot busy days and dead days at a glance.
  • Calls by hour of day — when your phones actually ring, in your timezone. Perfect for staffing decisions.
  • Outcomes — a donut of how calls ended: Completed, SIP Transfers, Internal Transfers, Voicemail, No Answer, Abandoned, Failed, or Other — with counts and percentages listed below it.
  • Top human agents — your five busiest agents ranked by calls handled, with their average handle time.
  • Group ring activity — how often the AI rang your permission groups and how many of those rings a human answered (appears when group transfers happened).
  • Dispositions logged — a tag cloud of the outcomes agents logged after their calls (appears when agents log dispositions).

images.caption: Call volume by day — all calls vs. connected, one bar pair per day.
Call volume by day — all calls vs. connected, one bar pair per day.
The Outcomes donut — how every call in the range ended.
The Outcomes donut — how every call in the range ended.

steps.title: Measure your team in Human Agents
steps.content:
Click Human Agents for the deepest view in the whole report — a full scorecard per agent. The KPI row totals it up: Active agents, Calls handled, Avg handle (AHT), Occupancy, Online time, Break time, Wrap-up (ACW), Logins, Total handle time, Calls supervised, Whisper / barge, Missed calls, and Rejected.

The Human agent performance table then breaks each agent down into column groups:

  • Handling — Handled (calls they took), Connect %, AHT, On call (total time), and Spoke % (calls where they actually talked).
  • Availability — Online, Idle, Break, Wrap-up (after-call work in the disposition picker), Occupancy (on-call time ÷ online time), Logins, and Last seen.
  • Group offers — group rings offered to the agent: Offered, Answered, Answer %, Rejected (manually declined), Solo miss (only agent rung, no action), Shared miss (several rung, nobody answered), and Missed calls.
  • Supervision — Monitor, Whisper, and Barge sessions, each shown as count · total time.
  • Dispositions — the outcomes the agent logged on their calls.
💡 Hover any column header for a plain-language definition, and click a header to sort the table by that metric.

images.caption: Human Agents: team KPI tiles plus a per-agent table with Handling, Availability, Group offers, and Supervision columns.
Human Agents: team KPI tiles plus a per-agent table with Handling, Availability, Group offers, and Supervision columns.

steps.title: Track AI performance in AI Agents
steps.content:
Click AI Agents to see how your AI workforce is performing. The KPI row shows AI agents, AI calls, Containment, Escalated to human, Connect rate, Avg handle, and Total cost.

The per-assistant table adds Calls, Connect %, Answer %, Voicemail %, Containment %, Escalated, SIP xfer, Avg talk, and Cost. Three definitions to know:

  • Containment — calls the AI handled fully on its own, without transferring out. Higher is better: it's the work your team never had to touch.
  • Escalated — calls the AI handed off to a human agent.
  • SIP xfer — calls the AI transferred to an external phone number.

images.caption: AI Agents: containment, escalations, transfers, talk time, and cost per assistant.
AI Agents: containment, escalations, transfers, talk time, and cost per assistant.

steps.title: Compare campaigns: Smart vs. Human
steps.content:
Two categories cover your outbound dialers:

  1. Smart Campaigns — AI dialer campaigns, with the same metric set as AI Agents (containment, escalations, SIP transfers, cost) but grouped by campaign. Perfect for A/B-comparing scripts or lists.
  2. Human Campaigns — predictive-dialer (Human Dialer) sessions grouped by dial list: Calls, Connect %, Answer %, Avg handle (mean operator time on a connected call), Total handle, and Cost.
💡 Not running campaigns yet? These tabs simply show an empty state until your first campaign dials out. See Create a Smart Dialer Campaign and Human Dialer to get started.

images.caption: Smart Campaigns: AI dialer performance grouped by campaign.
Smart Campaigns: AI dialer performance grouped by campaign.
Human Campaigns: predictive-dialer calls grouped by dial list, with operator handle time.
Human Campaigns: predictive-dialer calls grouped by dial list, with operator handle time.

steps.title: Audit hold times in Queue
steps.content:
Click Queue to review skill-group queue transfers — the calls a human agent parked in a group queue (with hold music) while skill-matched online agents were rung until one answered. KPI tiles: Queued calls, Answered, Timed out, Abandoned, Answer rate, Avg wait, and Longest wait.

The Queue calls table lists every queued call:

  • Queued / Customer — when it entered the queue and the caller's number.
  • Group / Skills — which permission group it waited in and any skill filter applied.
  • Status — answered, timeout, or abandoned (filterable from the column header).
  • Wait — how long the customer held before an answer or timeout.
  • Answered by / Queued by — who picked it up, and which agent parked it.
  • Matched — how many eligible agents were available.
  • On timeout — what happened when nobody answered: AI agent took over or Hang up.
  • Call — click Open to slide out the full call detail (recording, transcript, logs) without leaving the report.
⚠️ A creeping Longest wait or rising Timed out count is your early-warning signal to add agents to that group or tune the queue timeout — see Queue Settings.

images.caption: Queue: wait-time KPIs plus a per-call table with status, wait, and who answered.
Queue: wait-time KPIs plus a per-call table with status, wait, and who answered.

steps.title: Export any view to CSV
steps.content:
Every category exports with one click — and the file always matches what you're looking at:

  1. Open the category you want to export (for example Human Agents).
  2. Click Export (the download button in the top-right, next to Refresh).
  3. A CSV downloads instantly, named after the tab and date range — e.g. contact-center-human-agents-2026-06-10-to-2026-07-10.csv.

The columns are tab-specific: General exports the day-by-day volume, AI Agents/Smart Campaigns export the full per-assistant metric set, Human Campaigns export per-dial-list handle stats, and Human Agents export the complete scorecard — handling, availability, group offers, supervision, and top dispositions per agent. That's a board-ready report in one click.

💡 The export honors your current date range and filters, so you can produce a per-team or per-campaign file just by filtering first.

support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials

metaDescription: Master iMash Contact Center Reporting: six report categories (General, Human Agents, AI Agents, Smart Campaigns, Human Campaigns, Queue), every KPI explained, date presets, filters, and one-click CSV export.
